description: The nosuid mount option can be used to prevent execution of setuid programs in /srv. The SUID and SGID permissions should not be required in this directory. Add the nosuid option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /srv.
rationale:
The presence of SUID and SGID executables should be tightly controlled. The
<tt>/srv</tt> directory contains files served by various network services such as FTP. Users should
not be able to execute SUID or SGID binaries from this directory.
ocil_clause: the "/srv" file system does not have the "nosuid" option set
ocil:
Verify the nosuid option is configured for the /srv mount point, run the following command: 
$ sudo mount | grep '\s/srv\s'
. . . /srv . . . nosuid . . .
title: Add nosuid Option to /srv
